title: Restriction on number of deleted triples
description: I am using Virtuoso version 07.20.3237 and it seems that when trying to delete triples from a graph, only at most ResultSetMaxRows triples are deleted. E.g., executing sparql DELETE WHERE { graph <mygraph-uri> { ?p ?q ?r } } in isql will not delete all the triples in the graph if it contains more than ResultSetMaxRows triples. The same holds for more complex queries that delete parts of the graph.
poc:
for select * from (sparql select ?s ?p ?o WHERE {...}) dt do
  { sparql delete ... }
key_bug_features: Limit on deleted triples exceeding ResultSetMaxRows
bug_feature_seed: SPARQL PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> INSERT DATA { GRAPH <http://example.org/g> { ex:s1 ex:p ex:o1 . ex:s2 ex:p ex:o2 . ex:s3 ex:p ex:o3 . } };
SPARQL PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> INSERT DATA { GRAPH <http://example.org/g> { ex:s4 ex:p ex:o4 . ex:s5 ex:p ex:o5 . ex:s6 ex:p ex:o6 . } };
SPARQL PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> SELECT (COUNT(*) AS ?cnt) FROM <http://example.org/g> WHERE { ?s ?p ?o };
SPARQL PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> SELECT (COUNT(*) AS ?cnt) FROM <http://example.org/g> WHERE { ?s ?p ?o };
SPARQL PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> INSERT DATA { GRAPH <http://example.org/g> { ex:x1 ex:p ex:y1 . ex:x2 ex:p ex:y2 . ex:x3 ex:p ex:y3 . ex:x4 ex:p ex:y4 . } };
SPARQL PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> SELECT ?s ?p ?o FROM <http://example.org/g> WHERE { ?s ?p ?o };
SPARQL PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> WITH <http://example.org/g> DELETE { ?s ?p ?o } WHERE { ?s ?p ?o };
SPARQL PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> DELETE WHERE { GRAPH <http://example.org/g> { ?s ?p ?o } };
SPARQL PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> SELECT (COUNT(*) AS ?cnt) FROM <http://example.org/g> WHERE { ?s ?p ?o };
SPARQL PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> DELETE { ?s ?p ?o } WHERE { GRAPH <http://example.org/g> { ?s ?p ?o } };
SPARQL PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> SELECT (COUNT(*) AS ?cnt) FROM <http://example.org/g> WHERE { ?s ?p ?o };
